zh.lifeSummary: 當William Frederick Fox於1497年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾斯福可利時，他的父親Lord William Frederick Fox I47歲，母親Elizabeth Dunham45歲。他於大約1511年與Margaret Dorset Grey在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1558年2月在英國巴金哈姆爾格亞特 米森登享年61歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Frederick Fox于1497年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾斯福可利时，他的父亲Lord William Frederick Fox I47岁，母亲Elizabeth Dunham45岁。他于大约1511年与Margaret Dorset Grey在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1558年2月在英国巴金哈姆爾格亞特 米森登享年61岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Fox
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from a word denoting the animal (Middle English, Old English fox), widely used to denote a sly or cunning individual. It was also used for someone with red hair. In England this surname absorbed some early examples of surnames derived from the ancient Germanic personal names mentioned at Faulks and Foulks .
Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an tSionnaigh ‘son of the fox’ (see Tinney ).
Irish: also adopted for Ó Catharnaigh, see Kearney .

variants.name: Faulks
meanings: English: from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Fauque, Fauques, Fau(l)ques (oblique case Fau(l)que), originally an ancient Germanic byname Falco, meaning ‘falcon’.

variants.name: Foxman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Charna, Isadore, Shmuel.

variants.name: Fuchs
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Gerhard, Fritz, Mathias, Matthias, Armin, Ulrich, Erwin, Helmut.

German and Jewish: from Middle High German vuhs, German Fuchs ‘fox’, a nickname for a sly or cunning person, or for someone with red hair. This surname is also found in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic nicknames or surnames. Compare Fox 4, Fuks , and Futch .

variants.name: Kearney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Seamus, Caitlin, Declan, Dermot, Donovan, Eamonn, Liam.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Catharnaigh ‘descendant of Catharnach’, a personal name meaning ‘warlike’. Alternatively, it may be from Ó Cearnaigh or Mac Cearnaigh (from cearnach ‘victorious’) or from Ó Ceithearnaigh ‘descendant of Ceithearnach’, a personal name meaning ‘foot soldier’.

History: Most present-day bearers of this name are descended from Tadhg Ó Catharnaigh, killed in battle in 1084. He was nicknamed An Sionnach ‘the Fox’, and for this reason the Irish surname was sometimes Anglicized as Fox . Denis Kearney, born in Oakmont, Ireland, in 1847, was a US labor movement leader.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Frederick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Fredrick
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Fredric
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When William Frederick Fox was born in 1497, in Stewkley, Buckinghamshire, England, his father, Lord William Frederick Fox I, was 47 and his mother, Elizabeth Dunham, was 45. He married Margaret Dorset Grey about 1511, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in February 1558, in Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in London, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Frederick Fox est né en 1497, à Stewkley, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Lord William Frederick Fox I, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Dunham, avait 45 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Dorset Grey environ 1511, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en février 1558, à Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Frederick Fox 1497 in Stewkley, Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lord William Frederick Fox I 47 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Dunham 45. Er hat um 1511 in England, United Kingdom Margaret Dorset Grey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb im Februar 1558 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, England und wurde in London, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Frederick Fox è nato il nel 1497 nel Stewkley, Buckinghamshire, England, il suo padre, Lord William Frederick Fox I, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Dunham, aveva 45 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Dorset Grey circa 1511, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel febbraio 1558, nel Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, England, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a London, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Frederick Foxが1497年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアステウクリーで生まれた時，父Lord William Frederick Fox Iは47歳，母Elizabeth Dunhamは45歳であった。約1511年にイギリスイギリスでMargaret Dorset Greyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1558年2月にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアグレアト ミセンデンで61歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Frederick Fox(이)가 1497년 영국 벅잉함실르 스튜클리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord William Frederick Fox I(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Dunham(은)는 45세였다. 그는 대략 1511년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Margaret Dorset Grey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1558년 2월 영국 벅잉함실르 그리트 미센든에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Frederick Fox nasceu em 1497, em Stewkley, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Lord William Frederick Fox I, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Dunham, tinha 45 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Dorset Grey aproximadamente 1511, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em fevereiro de 1558, em Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Frederick Fox родился в 1497 г. в местности стюкли, буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Lord William Frederick Fox I было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Dunham, 45. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Dorset Grey около 1511 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер февраль 1558 г. в местности грит миссенден, буккингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Frederick Fox nació en 1497, en Stewkley, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Lord William Frederick Fox I, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Dunham, tenía 45 años. Se casó con Margaret Dorset Grey aproximadamente 1511, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en febrero de 1558, en Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
